core_takeaway: Agent_1 anchored Pencil successfully, but Agent_5's accepted Jewel anchor was worth much more.
why_or_counterexample: The first speaker shaped the stable allocation, yet that same allocation gave Agent_5 a 70-point Jewel while Agent_1 received a 44-point Pencil.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by anchoring Pencil, but the public discussion converged on Option C, assigning each agent a named anchor. Agent_5 publicly locked in Jewel as a top-value item and resisted later moves that would remove it.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Available: 5 entries. The private notes mostly treated Option C as the consensus, anchor-preserving baseline. Agent_1 accepted stabilization around Pencil, while Agent_5 prioritized keeping Jewel.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Available: Agents 1, 2, 4, and 5 submitted the same Option C allocation. Agent_3's divergent proposal concentrated all non-Pencil items with Agent_2 and did not become consensus.
phase_notes.private_voting: Available: 25 entries. The identical Option C proposals each received unanimous acceptance, while Agent_3's concentrated proposal was rejected by four agents.
phase_notes.reflection: Absent: no reflection section or reflection entries appear in the transcript.

mechanisms.explanation: The accepted allocation gave both endpoints their stated top items, but Agent_5's Jewel was worth 70 while Agent_1's Pencil was worth 44. The utility gap comes from value intensity, not from Agent_5 needing to dislodge Agent_1.

evidence.quote: Jewel is my top-value item by a large margin (70).

mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 did get a narrow first-position benefit: Pencil became a respected anchor and survived every consensus allocation. That helped Agent_1 avoid losing the top item, but it did not make Agent_1 beat Agent_5.

evidence.quote: preserves Pencil with Agent_1 as a solid anchor

mechanisms.theme: Agent_5 made Jewel non-negotiable inside the same consensus frame
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_5's late position did not need to overturn the earlier frame; it used the shared Option C frame to preserve Jewel. Because Jewel was Agent_5's dominant value source, preserving that anchor was enough to beat Agent_1.

evidence.quote: Securing Jewel in Round 1 is a powerful start for me.
Jewel must stay with me to preserve my top priority.
Jewel is by far my highest-valued item (70)

mechanisms.theme: Formal convergence made first and last proposals allocation-equivalent
mechanisms.explanation: The formal stage did not distinguish Agent_1's proposal from Agent_5's proposal: both used the same allocation. The system ultimately recorded unanimous acceptance of Proposal 5, but Proposal 1 had the same support and same allocation.

mechanisms.theme: Private votes protected each agent's own anchor rather than first-mover priority
mechanisms.explanation: Private voting shows agents accepted Option C because it protected their own high-value item. The only divergent proposal failed because it removed those anchors, especially Apple, Stone, and Jewel from their high-value holders.

evidence.quote: preserves fairness and my top-priority anchor.
my top priority Stone would be lost
